news.title:
Apar Industries surged 18.5%, from 7,997.8 to 9,476.85, outperforming Sensex's 1.5% gain.
The rally was driven by technical momentum, intraday gains, and analyst rating upgrades.
news.summary:
Apar Industries surged 18.5%, from 7,997.8 to 9,476.85, outperforming Sensex's 1.5% gain.
The rally was driven by technical momentum, intraday gains, and analyst rating upgrades.

news.title:
APAR Industries reported Q3 FY26 consolidated revenue of INR5,480 crore, up 16.2% YoY, driven by domestic growth and a favorable product mix. EBITDA rose 20.4% YoY to INR483 crore with margins at 8.8%.
Exports fell due to U.S. tariffs, but domestic revenue grew by 30%. Cable division exports declined significantly; however, INR500 crore orders were secured in Q3. Kavach project marks APAR's foray into railway safety.
news.summary:
APAR Industries reported Q3 FY26 consolidated revenue of INR5,480 crore, up 16.2% YoY, driven by domestic growth and a favorable product mix. EBITDA rose 20.4% YoY to INR483 crore with margins at 8.8%.
Exports fell due to U.S. tariffs, but domestic revenue grew by 30%. Cable division exports declined significantly; however, INR500 crore orders were secured in Q3. Kavach project marks APAR's foray into railway safety.

news.title:
Apar Industries and Waaree Energies may benefit from the India-US trade deal.
The agreement is expected to boost solar equipment companies in India.
news.summary:
Apar Industries and Waaree Energies may benefit from the India-US trade deal.
The agreement is expected to boost solar equipment companies in India.

news.title:
Apar Industries enters telecom via 156.9 Cr EPC contract for towers and fiber rollout.
Project supports Indian Railways' Kavach ATP system, with 2-year execution and 10 Cr working capital.
news.summary:
Apar Industries enters telecom via 156.9 Cr EPC contract for towers and fiber rollout.
Project supports Indian Railways' Kavach ATP system, with 2-year execution and 10 Cr working capital.

news.title:
Apar Industries shared Jan 2026 corporate presentation, highlighting leadership in aluminium conductors, transformer oils, and renewable cables.
FY25 revenue stood at ₹18,581 Cr ($2.2bn) with a 5-year CAGR of 30.6% and strategic roadmap details.
news.summary:
Apar Industries shared Jan 2026 corporate presentation, highlighting leadership in aluminium conductors, transformer oils, and renewable cables.
FY25 revenue stood at ₹18,581 Cr ($2.2bn) with a 5-year CAGR of 30.6% and strategic roadmap details.

news.title: Apar Industries reports 9MFY26 EBITDA per tonne at ₹42,311, exceeding ₹30,000 guidance.
news.summary: Apar Industries reports 9MFY26 EBITDA per tonne at ₹42,311, exceeding ₹30,000 guidance.

news.title: Apar Industries' Q3 revenue rose to 55B rupees from 47B YoY.
news.summary: Apar Industries' Q3 revenue rose to 55B rupees from 47B YoY.

news.title:
Apar Industries reports Q3 revenue of ₹5,479.7 cr and PBT of ₹313.1 cr.
9M FY26 PBT stands at ₹1,006.2 cr; ₹25 cr provision for Labour Code.
news.summary:
Apar Industries reports Q3 revenue of ₹5,479.7 cr and PBT of ₹313.1 cr.
9M FY26 PBT stands at ₹1,006.2 cr; ₹25 cr provision for Labour Code.

news.title: Apar Industries to review Q3 results on January 29.
news.summary: Apar Industries to review Q3 results on January 29.

news.title:
APAR Industries' board meeting on Jan 29, 2026, to review unaudited Q3 and 9-month results for FY25-26.
Trading window remains closed until Jan 31, 2026, as per SEBI insider trading regulations.
news.summary:
APAR Industries' board meeting on Jan 29, 2026, to review unaudited Q3 and 9-month results for FY25-26.
Trading window remains closed until Jan 31, 2026, as per SEBI insider trading regulations.

news.title:
Polycab India, Apar Industries, and Havells India are key players in renewable energy expansion, supplying essential equipment like cables, wires, and switchgear.
Polycab and Havells show strong growth and valuations, while Apar benefits from global traction; investors must weigh returns vs valuations carefully.
news.summary:
Polycab India, Apar Industries, and Havells India are key players in renewable energy expansion, supplying essential equipment like cables, wires, and switchgear.
Polycab and Havells show strong growth and valuations, while Apar benefits from global traction; investors must weigh returns vs valuations carefully.

news.title:
APAR Industries' CEO of Cable Solutions, Shashi Amin, resigns to pursue new opportunities.
Resignation effective from December 12, 2025; company acknowledges his contributions and wishes success.
news.summary:
APAR Industries' CEO of Cable Solutions, Shashi Amin, resigns to pursue new opportunities.
Resignation effective from December 12, 2025; company acknowledges his contributions and wishes success.

news.title:
Apar Industries appoints Mr. Chandrashekhar Shrotri as CEO of Cable Solutions from Dec 4, 2025.
He brings 35+ years of experience, previously serving as Segment CEO at Siemens for 22 countries.
news.summary:
Apar Industries appoints Mr. Chandrashekhar Shrotri as CEO of Cable Solutions from Dec 4, 2025.
He brings 35+ years of experience, previously serving as Segment CEO at Siemens for 22 countries.

news.title:
Apar Industries posts Rs 5,715.42 crore net sales and 40% operating profit growth.
Company shows resilience with solid fundamentals and positive results for 3 quarters.
news.summary:
Apar Industries posts Rs 5,715.42 crore net sales and 40% operating profit growth.
Company shows resilience with solid fundamentals and positive results for 3 quarters.

news.title:
Apar Industries enters dark fiber monetisation via PPP with Karnataka Power Transmission for 6,100 km network.
15-year deal includes Rs. 5 crore annual rental; 60-40 revenue share, boosting telecom infrastructure role.
news.summary:
Apar Industries enters dark fiber monetisation via PPP with Karnataka Power Transmission for 6,100 km network.
15-year deal includes Rs. 5 crore annual rental; 60-40 revenue share, boosting telecom infrastructure role.

news.title:
APAR Industries exceeded Q2 revenue forecasts by 8%, posting ₹57b, while EPS met analyst expectations at ₹204.
2026 forecasts show 2.5% revenue growth and 9.5% EPS increase, with price target up 14% to ₹9,973.
news.summary:
APAR Industries exceeded Q2 revenue forecasts by 8%, posting ₹57b, while EPS met analyst expectations at ₹204.
2026 forecasts show 2.5% revenue growth and 9.5% EPS increase, with price target up 14% to ₹9,973.

news.title:
APAR Industries shared audio recording of Q2FY26 analyst/investor call discussing unaudited financial results.
Recording is accessible on APAR's website as per SEBI LODR regulations, fulfilling compliance requirements.
news.summary:

news.title:

Growth driven by robust export performance.
news.summary:

news.title:
Apar Industries targets export scale-up with renewable conductors, high-margin cables.
Global OEM tie-ups aim to sustain double-digit growth and stable margins.

Focus areas include leadership in aluminium conductors, transformer oils, renewable cables, and future growth strategies.
